deep_dive.title: ERAlign: Energy-based Representation Alignment of GNNs and LLMs on Text-attributed Graphs
deep_dive.abstract: Text-attributed Graphs (TAGs) incorporate textual node attributes with graph structures to describe rich relational semantics. Recent efforts to integrate Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise for learning on TAGs, yet achieving well-aligned representations remains challenging. Prior studies largely rely on heuristics that perform coarse-grained matching. They lack sufficient constraints and ignore distributional alignment, leading to representation drift and limited generalization. Building on Energy-based Models (EBMs), we propose an Energy-based Representation Alignment (ERAlign) framework that projects GNN-encoded graph structure and LLM-derived text embeddings in a shared latent space to achieve distribution consistency. Concretely, layer-wise alignment is quantified by a distance metric and optimized via an EBM objective. By decreasing energy values, our framework yields well-aligned representations for downstream tasks. During training, we introduce Energy Discrepancy (ED) to avoid high sampling costs associated with intractable normalization. ED also carries theoretical guarantees of higher training efficiency and reduced energy landscape distortion. Empirical evaluations on eight TAG datasets demonstrate that ERAlign obtains state-of-the-art performance across varying levels of supervision and cross-task transfer scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: aligns GNN and LLM representations via energy-based model on text-attributed graphs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过能量模型对齐GNN和LLM在文本属性图上的表示
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过能量模型对齐GNN和LLM在文本属性图上的表示
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过能量模型对齐GNN和LLM在文本属性图上的表示
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: To address representation drift in prior GNN-LLM integration on text-attributed graphs, ERAlign uses energy-based models to project GNN graph structure and LLM text embeddings into a shared latent space with fine-grained alignment constraints, achieving better generalization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对文本属性图上GNN与LLM整合中表示对齐不足、分布差异忽略导致漂移的问题，ERAlign通过能量模型将GNN编码的图结构和LLM的文本嵌入投影到共享隐空间，利用对比散度和细粒度约束实现分布级对齐，在多个节点分类任务上验证了有效性，为图与语言模型的深度整合提供了新方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对文本属性图上GNN与LLM整合中表示对齐不足、分布差异忽略导致漂移的问题，ERAlign通过能量模型将GNN编码的图结构和LLM的文本嵌入投影到共享隐空间，利用对比散度和细粒度约束实现分布级对齐，在多个节点分类任务上验证了有效性，为图与语言模型的深度整合提供了新方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: integrating graph neural networks with large language models

deep_dive.title: Inference-Time Conformal Reasoning with Valid Factuality Control for Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly perform multi-step reasoning, where intermediate claims form implicit directed acyclic graphs whose node correctness is structurally conditioned on their ancestors. This makes factuality uncertainty structural, rather than a trivial accumulation of node-wise errors, and necessitates inference-time uncertainty quantification over the reasoning structure. While conformal prediction (CP) offers flexible user-specified factuality control, existing work remains post-hoc and cannot intervene during generation. To fill the gap between CP's flexibility and its post-hoc limitation, we propose an \emph{Inference-Time Conformal Reasoning (ITCR)} framework that integrates CP directly into reasoning graph generation. ITCR learns a structure-level factuality uncertainty function that aggregates claim-level factuality signals over reasoning graphs without complex modeling assumptions. We then design the non-conformity score based on graph-level factuality uncertainty and calibrate the conformal threshold to decide when to stop generation. We theoretically show such generation is nested, yielding valid coverage guarantees for factuality control. Experiments over multiple datasets and coverage objectives demonstrate empirically valid coverage. In downstream reasoning tasks, inference-time calibrated graphs yield more accurate generation than post-hoc pruned graphs.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Models LLM multi-step reasoning as implicit DAGs and applies conformal prediction for structural factuality control, directly addressing LLM graph reasoning.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将LLM多步推理建模为隐式有向无环图并应用共形预测进行结构事实性控制，直接探索LLM的图推理能力。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将LLM多步推理建模为隐式有向无环图并应用共形预测进行结构事实性控制，直接探索LLM的图推理能力。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将LLM多步推理建模为隐式有向无环图并应用共形预测进行结构事实性控制，直接探索LLM的图推理能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LLMs' chain-of-thought forms implicit DAGs where errors propagate; ITCR integrates conformal prediction at inference time to control factuality with user-specified guarantees, improving reasoning reliability on graph-structured thought processes.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: LLM的链式推理形成隐式有向无环图，节点正确性受祖先影响。ITCR在推理过程中嵌入共形预测，实现可保证的事实性控制，提升了LLM在图结构思维链上的推理可靠性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: LLM的链式推理形成隐式有向无环图，节点正确性受祖先影响。ITCR在推理过程中嵌入共形预测，实现可保证的事实性控制，提升了LLM在图结构思维链上的推理可靠性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: graph reasoning capabilities of large language models

quick_skim.title: A Training-Free Mixture-of-Agents Framework for Multi-Document Summarization using LLMs and Knowledge Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-Document Summarization (MDS) plays a critical role in distilling essential information from collections of textual data. Existing approaches often struggle to capture complex inter-document relationships, rely heavily on large amounts of labeled data for supervised training, or exhibit limited generalization across domains and languages. To address these limitations, we present a training-free mixture-of-agents framework for MDS that leverages the complementary strengths of large language models (LLMs) and knowledge graphs. Our approach decomposes summarization into specialized agent tasks: extractive selection, knowledge-aware abstraction, and iterative refinement, each operating without task-specific fine-tuning. We unify their outputs using a multi-perspective consistency mechanism guided by LLMs. Experiments across four datasets in English and Vietnamese demonstrate state-of-the-art or competitive performance, validating the effectiveness and adaptability of our modular design.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Combines LLMs and knowledge graphs via a mixture-of-agents for multi-document summarization, integrating graph-structured knowledge into LLM workflow.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过混合代理框架融合LLM和知识图谱进行多文档摘要，将图结构知识融入LLM工作流。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过混合代理框架融合LLM和知识图谱进行多文档摘要，将图结构知识融入LLM工作流。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过混合代理框架融合LLM和知识图谱进行多文档摘要，将图结构知识融入LLM工作流。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a training-free framework where specialized agents use LLMs and knowledge graphs for extractive selection, knowledge-aware abstraction, and refinement, achieving strong MDS without supervised data, demonstrating effective graph-LLM synergy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出无需训练的混合代理框架，利用LLM和知识图谱分工协作完成多文档摘要，并在未见过的数据上表现优异，展现了图与LLM协同的优势。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 提出无需训练的混合代理框架，利用LLM和知识图谱分工协作完成多文档摘要，并在未见过的数据上表现优异，展现了图与LLM协同的优势。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: using large language models for graph-structured data

quick_skim.title: Reducing Hallucinations in Complex Question Answering using Simple Graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (long version)
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have fundamentally transformed the landscape of Natural Language Processing. Despite these advances, LLMs and LLM-based systems remain prone to a variety of failure modes. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems have emerged as a common deployment scenario seeking to both avoid the well known risk of the LLM "hallucinating" information, and to enable reasoning and question answering over proprietary information that the LLM did not have access to during training without resorting to expensive model fine-tuning.   In this work, we explore the idea of using a lightweight graph structure with a relatively simple graph schema, to support the RAG subsystem via a dedicated toolset. We design an agentic system with a variety of vector search and graph query tools operating over a structured dataset based on a curated subset of English Wikipedia articles, and evaluate its performance on questions from MoNaCo, a challenging Wikipedia QA benchmark of complex query answering tasks.   Our results show that the introduction of graph-based tools can significantly increase the precision and recall of factual correctness, can halve the number of hallucinated answers, and achieves the highest fine-grained truthfulness score among the three evaluated scenarios. All this with a modest increase in token usage.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Leverages a graph-based retrieval structure to augment LLM QA, integrating graph indexing with LLM generation to reduce hallucinations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用基于图的检索结构增强LLM问答，融合图索引与LLM生成以降低幻觉。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用基于图的检索结构增强LLM问答，融合图索引与LLM生成以降低幻觉。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用基于图的检索结构增强LLM问答，融合图索引与LLM生成以降低幻觉。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a simple graph schema for RAG to provide structured context, improving complex QA accuracy and reducing hallucinations; showcases a practical graph-LLM integration in a retrieval setting.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出用轻量级图结构支撑检索增强生成，为复杂问答提供结构化上下文，有效降低LLM幻觉。该方法展示了在图增强LLM应用中的可行方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 提出用轻量级图结构支撑检索增强生成，为复杂问答提供结构化上下文，有效降低LLM幻觉。该方法展示了在图增强LLM应用中的可行方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 图大型语言模型, especially work that connects or combines: integrating graph neural networks with large language models; using large language models for graph-structured data; graph reasoning capabilities of large language models; graph representation learning combined with large language models; graph machine learning techniques for large language models; graph-enhanced training of large language models; How to integrate graph neural networks with large language models?; Training graph enhanced large language models.
